advertise_mode.description: Determines the advertisement mode for the peer, either 'DEFAULT' to advertise a standard set of routes or 'CUSTOM' to specify a custom set of advertised routes.

advertised_groups.description: Defines the list of predefined groups of IP ranges to advertise, such as 'ALL_SUBNETS'. Must match one of the available groups supported by Google Cloud.

advertised_ip_ranges.description: Defines the IP ranges advertised to the peer, specified as CIDR blocks. Must be non-overlapping and valid IP ranges.

advertised_route_priority.description: Defines the priority of advertised routes, where lower values indicate higher priority. Must be an integer between 0 and 65535.

bfd.description: Configures Bidirectional Forwarding Detection (BFD) for the peer; requires BFD to be supported and enabled on both ends of the connection.

custom_learned_ip_ranges.description: Defines custom IP ranges to be learned and advertised by the peer, must be valid CIDR blocks.

custom_learned_route_priority.description: Defines the priority for custom learned routes, determining their preference in routing decisions. Must be an integer between 0 and 65535.

enable.description: Determines whether the BGP peer is enabled. Must be set to true or false.

enable_ipv4.description: Enables IPv4 support for the router peer; set to true to allow IPv4 traffic.

enable_ipv6.description: Controls whether IPv6 is enabled for the peer; must be set to true to allow IPv6 traffic.

export_policies.description: Defines the export policies for the router peer, specifying which routes are advertised to the peer.

import_policies.description: Defines the set of policy names to control the import of routes into the router, ensuring route selection complies with specified constraints.

interface.description: Specifies the interface name used by the router peer, which must match an existing router interface.

ip_address.description: Specifies the IP address of the BGP peer. Must be a valid IPv4 address within the subnet range.

ipv4_nexthop_address.description: Specifies the IPv4 address of the next hop for the BGP peer; must be a valid IPv4 address.

ipv6_nexthop_address.description: IPv6 address of the next hop, required for IPv6 BGP peer configuration.

md5_authentication_key.description: Specifies the MD5 authentication key for BGP sessions, must be a string between 1-80 characters.

name.description: Unique name of the peer, required for identifying the BGP session within the router.

peer_asn.description: Specifies the autonomous system number for the BGP peer; must be a valid ASN within the range of 1 to 4294967295.

peer_ip_address.description: IP address of the peer router interface, must be in the same subnet as the router interface IP.

peer_ipv4_nexthop_address.description: IPv4 address of the next hop for the peer connection, used for routing traffic; must be within the range of the router's subnetwork.

peer_ipv6_nexthop_address.description: IPv6 address of the next hop for the peer; must be in the same subnet and unique within the network.

router.description: Defines the name or self-link of the Google Compute Router to associate with the peer.

router_appliance_instance.description: Specifies the self-link of the VM instance to use as the router appliance, must be in the same region as the router.

timeouts.description: Defines the time durations for create, update, and delete operations of the router peer, allowing customization of operation timeouts.

zero_advertised_route_priority.description: Defines the priority for routes advertised to the peer, with lower values indicating higher priority. Must be a non-negative integer.

zero_custom_learned_route_priority.description: Specifies the priority of custom learned routes when no priority is explicitly set, must be a value between 0 and 65535.
